name: Florida Certified Nursing Assistant Certification
description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Florida - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.description: Florida Board of Nursing, 4052 Bald Cypress Way, BIN C-02, Tallahassee, FL 32399-3252

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Training
requiredDocuments.description: Certificate from your state-approved CNA program, or high school diploma/GED if using the Challenger Route.

requiredDocuments.name: Prometric Exam Results
requiredDocuments.description: Official results from your Prometric competency exam.

requiredDocuments.name: Level II Background Screening
requiredDocuments.description: Completed via AHCA Clearinghouse (flclearinghouse.com) using ORI: EDOH0380Z.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: AHCA Clearinghouse (CHAI) at flclearinghouse.com — ORI: EDOH0380Z

mainEntity.name: Can I challenge the CNA exam in Florida without training?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Florida's Challenger Route lets individuals 18 or older with a high school diploma take the CNA exam without completing a formal training program.

mainEntity.name: Who administers the Florida CNA exam?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Florida CNA competency exam is administered by Prometric. Register at prometric.com/nurseaide/fl.

mainEntity.name: How do I verify a Florida CNA license?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use the FL DOH MQA Provider Search at mqa-internet.doh.state.fl.us/MQASearchServices/HealthCareProviders.

mainEntity.name: How do I transfer my CNA to Florida?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply for endorsement through the FL Health Source portal (flhealthsource.gov). You must hold an active out-of-state CNA certificate and pass a Level II background check.

mainEntity.name: How long does Florida CNA endorsement take?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications are processed in 2 to 4 weeks once your file is complete.
